world_assessment: The dominant force this week is the rate-driven reflation rotation persisting from prior weeks. Rising Treasury yields continue to punish high-multiple growth tech (NVDA below SMA50, MSFT MACD bearish, TSM OBV falling) while turbocharging cyclicals and financials (DE +17.9%, F +11.2%, GS +6.1% with strong institutional momentum). The two standout catalysts: Goldman Sachs restarting its crypto trading desk, affirming our Financial Re-rating thesis with unexpected upside optionality; and Wedbush raising MU price target to $100, validating our semiconductor thesis even as the sector consolidates technically. The market is not breaking down — it is rotating. News flow was thin this week (no major macro surprises, no Fed communication shifts), which means the rate regime is the dominant variable. With 13 trades executed and our portfolio already well-aligned to the rotation, this is a week to hold convictions and let winners run rather than force new entries.

thesis_updates.notes: Technically weak: RSI=43, below SMA50, MACD bearish, ADX=20, OBV flat. This is a rate-driven multiple compression in high-growth tech, not a fundamental breakdown. The AI/cloud/datacenter demand thesis is entirely unchanged — no analyst downgrades, no hyperscaler capex cuts, no supply normalization news. ADX=20 means no strong downtrend is confirmed. Holding per Lesson 3 — thesis intact, price weakness is noise. Exit only if ADX confirms a downtrend >30 with sustained OBV distribution AND a fundamental crack (customer capex guidance cuts).

RSI=46, MACD bearish, OBV falling — technically weakening in line with sector rotation away from growth. Structural thesis completely intact: TSMC remains the irreplaceable chokepoint of global chip supply, and ADX=29 still indicates trend persistence without confirming reversal. OBV falling is the most concerning signal — watching for sustained institutional distribution vs near-term tactical selling. Hold. Thesis invalidation would require evidence of competitor fab catch-up (Samsung 3nm yields improving meaningfully) or demand destruction.

Best-positioned semiconductor name technically: RSI=58, MACD bullish, ADX=30, OBV rising. Wedbush raising PT to $100 confirms sell-side is catching up to our thesis — we entered at $80.72, current $91.09 with analyst coverage now driving momentum. Memory shortage thesis strengthening as AI workload and datacenter build-outs consume DRAM capacity. No action needed — this is our cleanest semiconductor hold with +12.8% P&L and a runway to $105 target.

SHORT position: MACD has turned bullish — a yellow flag requiring heightened vigilance. However, OBV is still falling (institutional distribution ongoing), RSI=41 (not oversold extreme), still below SMA50. The MACD cross appears to be a technical bounce in a fundamentally broken stock rather than a thesis reversal. Cord-cutting, 5G capex burden, T-Mobile competitive pressure, and rising debt costs remain intact as structural headwinds. Hold short with elevated monitoring — thesis invalidation trigger: VZ reclaims SMA50 with RSI breaking above 55 on rising OBV.

Most ambiguous technical setup in portfolio: RSI=55 (neutral), above SMA50 (positive), MACD bearish (negative), ADX=15 (weak — below 20 means no confirmed trend), OBV rising (institutional accumulation ongoing). The ADX=15 is the key concern — no strong trend in either direction. However, rising OBV suggests smart money is not distributing despite rate headwinds. Structural thesis (networking ASIC monopoly, hyperscaler dependency, 5G silicon) unchanged. Hold — would be a sell candidate if OBV turns negative.

RSI=69, MACD bullish, ADX=35, OBV rising — thesis executing perfectly. Blue Wave infrastructure + precision agriculture story is the textbook case study for our investment framework. +17.9% P&L. RSI=69 approaching extended territory but ADX=35 with rising OBV confirms this is institutional trend-following, not a blow-off. Any RSI pullback toward 55-60 would be an add opportunity. Hold — target $365 remains in play.

RSI=69, near upper Bollinger Band, MACD bullish, ADX=45, OBV rising — the strongest momentum profile in the portfolio alongside GS. Near upper BB signals short-term consolidation likely. +11.2% P&L. EV transition + Blue Wave thesis intact with no contradicting news. ADX=45 means any BB consolidation is a pause, not a reversal. Hold full position through any near-term digestion.

RSI=48, MACD bearish, ADX=26, OBV falling — under pressure from the rate rotation. Down 3.5% from entry. This is the portfolio's most vulnerable position to the current regime: high-multiple tech with duration risk in a rising rate environment. However, the structural thesis (Azure growth, enterprise AI, federal cloud contracts) is undiminished. ADX=26 does not confirm a strong downtrend. OBV falling is the key watch — if institutional distribution accelerates below ADX=20 with OBV trend firmly negative, thesis re-evaluation warranted. Hold for now.

RSI=71, MACD bullish, ADX=46, OBV rising — the best technical setup in the portfolio. Goldman restarting its crypto trading desk (Bitcoin futures and non-deliverable forwards for clients starting next week) is a direct positive catalyst: adds trading revenue from institutional crypto adoption, expands their addressable market beyond traditional capital markets, and signals management conviction in capitalizing on the digital asset cycle. This is additive to our original thesis, not a distraction from it. GS at $330.94 vs $311.96 entry (+6.1%) with target $375 — thesis is strengthening, not weakening. Hold.

theme_updates.name: Financial Re-rating
theme_updates.reason: Goldman Sachs restarting the crypto desk signals major banks are expanding into digital assets — a growth optionality layer beyond the net interest margin / rate thesis. Square's industrial bank beginning operations further demonstrates financial sector innovation velocity. The re-rating thesis is broader than originally scoped: banks are not just benefiting from rates, they are actively expanding into high-growth adjacencies (crypto, fintech infrastructure). Upgrading conviction from 3 to 4.

theme_updates.name: Reflation/Rates Rotation
theme_updates.reason: Portfolio performance data this week provides definitive validation: cyclicals (DE +17.9%, F +11.2%) and financials (GS +6.1%, MU +12.8%) are outperforming meaningfully while growth tech (MSFT -3.5%, NVDA barely positive) faces compression. External confirmation: CAT RSI=65/ADX=40, JPM RSI=66/ADX=41, XOM RSI=66/ADX=35 — the entire cyclical complex is in confirmed institutional uptrends. This rotation is structural, not tactical. Upgrading from 3 to 4.

lesson_updates.reason: NVDA and TSM are both technically weak this week (below SMA50 or MACD bearish) but their structural theses are entirely intact. Lesson 3 prescribed holding — and that is the correct call. Meanwhile MU, with rising OBV and MACD bullish despite sector-wide selling pressure, is demonstrating exactly the institutional accumulation pattern Lesson 3 describes. The lesson continues to provide correct guidance in the rate-rotation environment.

belief_updates.name: Thesis Integrity Over Price Action
belief_updates.description: When a position is in drawdown, the exit decision must be driven by thesis invalidation — not price movement. Before considering an exit, verify three conditions: (1) has the original structural thesis changed materially? (2) is ADX >25 with OBV rising, confirming institutional accumulation despite price weakness? (3) has contradicting fundamental news emerged? If the thesis is intact and institutions are still accumulating, price weakness is noise. Exit only when the thesis breaks, not when the price is lower. This principle does NOT mean 'never sell losers' — it requires active, ongoing verification that thesis conditions hold.

weekly_summary: Week of Mar 2, 2021: Light news week dominated by portfolio confirmation signals rather than new catalysts. Goldman restarting crypto desk adds optionality to our Financial Re-rating thesis. Wedbush raises MU PT to $100, validating our semiconductor memory thesis. Reflation rotation persists: cyclical longs (DE +17.9%, F +11.2%, GS +6.1%) outperforming while growth tech (MSFT -3.5%) faces rate headwinds. No new positions opened — over-trading discipline maintained at 13 total trades. Upgraded Financial Re-rating and Reflation/Rates Rotation themes to conviction 4. Established first portfolio Belief: Thesis Integrity Over Price Action, derived from Lesson 3 (score now 6). Portfolio +5.3% vs S&P +3.0%, maintaining +2.3% alpha. Strategy: patient, thesis-driven, letting winners run while monitoring MSFT and AVGO technical deterioration closely.
